description_long: This is a tall, multi-tiered pagoda with six distinct stories, each separated by wide, overhanging eaves that curve upward at the corners. The structure is octagonal in plan, with each level featuring a green-tiled roof and red-brown supporting columns and beams. The base is surrounded by a patterned stone platform, which extends outward in a decorative geometric shape. At the apex of the pagoda is an ornate finial, consisting of a spire with decorative elements and cables extending to the roof corners. The windows and doorways are small and regularly spaced, typical of traditional East Asian pagoda architecture. The overall appearance is grand and ornamental, suggesting a historical or cultural landmark.
description: A tall, six-story octagonal pagoda with green-tiled, upward-curving roofs and an ornate spire, set on a decorative stone platform.
description_view_0: Front view showing the central entrance, six stacked roof tiers, and the ornate finial at the top.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the symmetry of the octagonal structure and the layered, upturned eaves.
description_view_2: Rear view with a clear view of the back platform and the repeating roof pattern.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, similar to view 1, emphasizing the multi-tiered roof and decorative base.
